ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: O you who covers himself [with a garment],
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: O you covered up ˹in your clothes˺!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Arise and warn
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Arise and warn ˹all˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And your Lord glorify
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Revere your Lord ˹alone˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And your clothing purify
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Purify your garments.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And uncleanliness avoid
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Continue to˺ shun idols.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And do not confer favor to acquire more
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do not do a favour expecting more ˹in return˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But for your Lord be patient.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And persevere for ˹the sake of˺ your Lord.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when the trumpet is blown,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹For˺ when the Trumpet will be sounded,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That Day will be a difficult day
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: that will ˹truly˺ be a difficult Day—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: For the disbelievers - not easy.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: far from easy for the disbelievers.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Leave Me with the one I created alone
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And leave to me ˹O Prophet˺ the one I created all by Myself,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And to whom I granted extensive wealth
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and granted him abundant wealth,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And children present [with him]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and children always by his side,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And spread [everything] before him, easing [his life].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and made life very easy for him.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then he desires that I should add more.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Yet he is hungry for more.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No! Indeed, he has been toward Our verses obstinate.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But no! ˹For˺ he has been truly stubborn with Our revelations.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: I will cover him with arduous torment.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I will make his fate unbearable,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, he thought and deliberated.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: for he contemplated and determined ˹a degrading label for the Quran˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So may he be destroyed [for] how he deliberated.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: May he be condemned! How evil was what he determined!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then may he be destroyed [for] how he deliberated.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: May he be condemned even more! How evil was what he determined!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then he considered [again];
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then he re-contemplated ˹in frustration˺,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then he frowned and scowled;
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then frowned and scowled,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then he turned back and was arrogant
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then turned his back ˹on the truth˺ and acted arrogantly,

ayahs.textArabic: فَقَالَ إِنۡ هَٰذَآ إِلَّا سِحۡرٞ يُؤۡثَرُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faqala in hatha illa sihrun yutharu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And said, "This is not but magic imitated [from others].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: saying, “This ˹Quran˺ is nothing but magic from the ancients.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: This is not but the word of a human being."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: This is no more than the word of a man.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: I will drive him into Saqar.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Soon I will burn him in Hell!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And what can make you know what is Saqar?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And what will make you realize what Hell is?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: It lets nothing remain and leaves nothing [unburned],
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It does not let anyone live or die,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Blackening the skins.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: scorching the skin.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Over it are nineteen [angels].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It is overseen by nineteen ˹keepers˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا جَعَلۡنَآ أَصۡحَٰبَ ٱلنَّارِ إِلَّا مَلَٰٓئِكَةٗۖ وَمَا جَعَلۡنَا عِدَّتَهُمۡ إِلَّا فِتۡنَةٗ لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ لِيَسۡتَيۡقِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُواْ ٱلۡكِتَٰبَ وَيَزۡدَادَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓاْ إِيمَٰنٗا وَلَا يَرۡتَابَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُواْ ٱلۡكِتَٰبَ وَٱلۡمُؤۡمِنُونَ وَلِيَقُولَ ٱلَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٞ وَٱلۡكَٰفِرُونَ مَاذَآ أَرَادَ ٱللَّهُ بِهَٰذَا مَثَلٗاۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُضِلُّ ٱللَّهُ مَن يَشَآءُ وَيَهۡدِي مَن يَشَآءُۚ وَمَا يَعۡلَمُ جُنُودَ رَبِّكَ إِلَّا هُوَۚ وَمَا هِيَ إِلَّا ذِكۡرَىٰ لِلۡبَشَرِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama jaAAalna ashaba alnnari illa malaikatan wama jaAAalna AAiddatahum illa fitnatan lillatheena kafaroo liyastayqina allatheena ootoo alkitaba wayazdada allatheena amanoo eemanan wala yartaba allatheena ootoo alkitaba waalmuminoona waliyaqoola allatheena fee quloobihim maradun waalkafiroona matha arada Allahu bihatha mathalan kathalika yudillu Allahu man yashao wayahdee man yashao wama yaAAlamu junooda rabbika illa huwa wama hiya illa thikra lilbashari
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We have not made the keepers of the Fire except angels. And We have not made their number except as a trial for those who disbelieve - that those who were given the Scripture will be convinced and those who have believed will increase in faith and those who were given the Scripture and the believers will not doubt and that those in whose hearts is hypocrisy and the disbelievers will say, "What does Allah intend by this as an example?" Thus does Allah leave astray whom He wills and guides whom He wills. And none knows the soldiers of your Lord except Him. And mention of the Fire is not but a reminder to humanity.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We have appointed only ˹stern˺ angels as wardens of the Fire. And We have made their number only as a test for the disbelievers, so that the People of the Book will be certain, and the believers will increase in faith, and neither the People of the Book nor the believers will have any doubts, and so that those ˹hypocrites˺ with sickness in their hearts and the disbelievers will argue, “What does Allah mean by such a number?” In this way Allah leaves whoever He wills to stray and guides whoever He wills. And none knows the forces of your Lord except He. And this ˹description of Hell˺ is only a reminder to humanity.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No! By the moon
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But no! By the moon,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [by] the night when it departs
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the night as it retreats,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [by] the morning when it brightens,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the day as it breaks!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, the Fire is of the greatest [afflictions]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely Hell is one of the mightiest catastrophes—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: As a warning to humanity -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: a warning to humankind,

ayahs.textArabic: لِمَن شَآءَ مِنكُمۡ أَن يَتَقَدَّمَ أَوۡ يَتَأَخَّرَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Liman shaa minkum an yataqaddama aw yataakhkhara
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: To whoever wills among you to proceed or stay behind.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: to whichever of you chooses to take the lead or lag behind.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Every soul, for what it has earned, will be retained
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Every soul will be detained for what it has done,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except the companions of the right,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: except the people of the right,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Who will be] in gardens, questioning each other
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: who will be in Gardens, asking one another

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: About the criminals,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: about the wicked ˹who will then be asked˺:

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [And asking them], "What put you into Saqar?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: “What has landed you in Hell?”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will say, "We were not of those who prayed,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will reply, “We were not of those who prayed,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Nor did we used to feed the poor.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: nor did we feed the poor.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And we used to enter into vain discourse with those who engaged [in it],
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We used to indulge ˹in falsehood˺ along with others,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And we used to deny the Day of Recompense
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and deny the Day of Judgment,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Until there came to us the certainty."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: until the inevitable came to us.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So there will not benefit them the intercession of [any] intercessors.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So the pleas of intercessors will be of no benefit to them.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then what is [the matter] with them that they are, from the reminder, turning away
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Now, what is the matter with them that they are turning away from the reminder,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: As if they were alarmed donkeys
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: as if they were spooked zebras

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Fleeing from a lion?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: fleeing from a lion?

ayahs.textArabic: بَلۡ يُرِيدُ كُلُّ ٱمۡرِيٕٖ مِّنۡهُمۡ أَن يُؤۡتَىٰ صُحُفٗا مُّنَشَّرَةٗ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Bal yureedu kullu imriin minhum an yuta suhufan munashsharatan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Rather, every person among them desires that he would be given scriptures spread about.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In fact, each one of them wishes to be given a ˹personal˺ letter ˹from Allah˺ for all ˹to read˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No! But they do not fear the Hereafter.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But no! In fact, they do not fear the Hereafter.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No! Indeed, the Qur'an is a reminder
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Enough! Surely this ˹Quran˺ is a reminder.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then whoever wills will remember it.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So let whoever wills be mindful of it.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا يَذۡكُرُونَ إِلَّآ أَن يَشَآءَ ٱللَّهُۚ هُوَ أَهۡلُ ٱلتَّقۡوَىٰ وَأَهۡلُ ٱلۡمَغۡفِرَةِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama yathkuroona illa an yashaa Allahu huwa ahlu alttaqwa waahlu almaghfirati
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they will not remember except that Allah wills. He is worthy of fear and adequate for [granting] forgiveness.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But they cannot do so unless Allah wills. He ˹alone˺ is worthy to be feared and entitled to forgive.
